lesson.menu_title: If Only — Talking About Regret and What You Wish Were Different
lesson.menu_desc: Wish and if only (I wish I had asked sooner / If only I hadn't doubted it / I wish they would decide) to reflect on the founder journey with honesty -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- If Only
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 15 -- If Only: Talking About Regret and What You Wish Were Different

listenings.text: Hi Eliane, it is Henry. I have been thinking about our conversation. Every founder I have backed says the same thing in hindsight: I wish I had trusted myself sooner. When you hit a setback, the temptation is to freeze and to doubt every decision. But the founders who last are the ones with resilience. They bounce back and treat each mistake as a wake-up call, not a reason to quit. Do not fall into the blind spot of thinking you must know everything. I wish I had learned that earlier myself. If only someone had told me at the start: the learning curve never really ends, so stop wishing it were easier and start using what each setback teaches you. Trust your gut feeling. It is usually right.

Hi team, it is Eliane. I have been reflecting on our first year, and I want to share a few honest thoughts. Looking back, I wish I had asked for help sooner. I carried too much alone, and the learning curve was steeper than it needed to be. If only I had trusted my gut feeling on the pilot, we would have moved faster. There were setbacks where I almost decided to cut our losses, and days of real self-doubt. But we bounced back, every time. In hindsight, each hard moment was a wake-up call that made us stronger. The biggest trade-off is still growth versus control, and I sometimes wish the board would move faster. But I would not undo the journey. I only wish I had believed in us a little earlier.
